subject: Technical Drawing (WASSCE)
document_title: West African Senior School Certificate Examination Technical Drawing Syllabus

paper_1.description: Objective/Geometrical Drawing consisting of Sections A and B.
paper_1.section_a: 40 compulsory multiple choice questions for 40 marks in 1 hour. Part I: 30 questions on general principles, techniques, uses of plane and solid geometry. Part II: Two alternatives IIA (Building Drawing) and IIB (Mechanical Drawing), 10 questions (31-40). Candidates answer either part IIA or IIB.
paper_1.section_b: 4 questions on plane and solid geometry carrying 60 marks in 1 1/2 hours. Candidates answer 3 questions.

paper_2.description: Short Structured Questions and Practical Drawing consisting of Sections A and B.
paper_2.section_a: Two parts, I and II. Candidates answer either part for 30 marks in 45 minutes. Part I (not available in Ghana): 10 structured questions. Part II (not available in Nigeria): 3 questions requiring sketches, candidates answer two (Q1 and any other).
paper_2.section_b: Two questions: one each on Building Drawing and Mechanical Drawing. Candidates answer either question in 1 3/4 hours for 70 marks.

preliminaries.notes: Pictorial and orthographic. Uses and care of equipment.
Types of lines and their uses. Types and styles of lettering. Title blocks, Border lines (conform to BS, ISO). Dimensioning: Principles and techniques.

plane_geometry.content: Division of lines into a given number of equal parts or proportion and its applications
Plain and Diagonal scales and their uses including scale of chords

plane_geometry.notes: Exercises involving the use of protractor and a pair of compasses.

plane_geometry.content: Tangency involving circles, arcs and lines
plane_geometry.notes: Blending of lines and curves. Mutually tangential circles - internal and external. Application to spanners, anchors, tools, and parts.
Equivalent and proportional figures determined either by area or sides.
Construction of straight lines, circles, ellipses, parabolas, hyperbolas, helices, trochoids (inferior and superior), spirals, cycloids, hypocycloids, epicycloids, involutes and link mechanisms.

solid_geometry.notes: Approximate methods for drawing oblique and isometric curves. Isometric scale, dimensioning cabinet, cavalier etc. One and two points perspective. Isometric circles.
Prisms, cones, cylinders, pyramids (right or oblique) or combinations of them (including second auxiliary projection).

solid_geometry.content: Traces of a line, true length of straight lines, points and line relationship
solid_geometry.notes: Location of points and lines in space, true lengths and true angles, intersecting lines, plane relationships, types of planes and edges, views of a plane, true shape of lamina.
Including application to plane figures and intersecting planes. True shapes of sections of prisms, cylinders, pyramids and cones (ellipse, parabola, hyperbola, circle, triangle).
Surfaces of right and oblique pyramids, prisms, cylinders and cones and their frustums. Transition pieces.

building_drawing.notes: Pictorial and orthographic sketches of building details and tools used in building industries. Recommendations of current BS 1192 (metric).

building_drawing.content: Intersection of straight and curved moulding in the same plane
building_drawing.notes: Including determination of the true shape of mitres.
Semi-elliptical, segmental, semi-circular, tudor, equilateral parabolic etc.
Flat, pitched, gabbled, butterfly, lean-to, etc. Development of roof-surfaces and determination of bevels.
Simple houses, garages, sheds, etc. Dimensioning, lettering, conventional representation of materials and fittings.

building_drawing.content: Constructional details of parts of buildings
building_drawing.notes: Foundations, walls and openings, jambs, sills, lintels, roofs, doors, windows, staircases (half-turn, dog-leg, straight-flight), frames, linings, architraves, floors, simple reinforcement for columns, beams, piers, slabs etc.
Sectional views and the use of different scales. Up to 2 floors only.

mechanical_drawing.notes: Sketches of engineering components, tools, electrical circuits, plant layouts. Recommendations of current BS 308A (metric).
Plumbing and welding symbols. Emphasis on correct techniques.

mechanical_drawing.content: Fasteners and locking devices - conventional representations
mechanical_drawing.notes: Permanent and temporary joints e.g. welded and riveted joints, bolts and nuts, screws, studs, splined shafts, cotters, collars, keys etc.
Screw threads and springs (both left and right handed).

mechanical_drawing.content: Working and assembling drawing and details in both first and third angles projections
mechanical_drawing.notes: Dimensioning, reference balloon, parts list, title blocks, lettering and good draughtsmanship.
Full, half, part, offset, broken, removed, aligned, revolved sections etc.
